prompt: On my Jekyll site I have `_posts/*.md` and I want each post page to show: (a) estimated reading time, (b) a table of contents built from the post's H2 and H3 headings, (c) related posts that share at least one tag. I'm on Jekyll 4.4 and use kramdown. Where do I put this and what's the actual code?

assertions.text: Reading time uses Jekyll's built-in `number_of_words` filter (or `page.content | split | size`) rather than a heavy custom plugin
TOC uses kramdown's native `* TOC\n{:toc}` (or `{:toc}`) — does NOT introduce jekyll-toc or unrelated plugins
Restricts the TOC to H2 and H3 (e.g., `toc_levels: 2..3` in _config.yml)
Related posts uses a correct tag-overlap algorithm (concat + uniq size comparison, or a where_exp/contains check) — logic actually finds overlapping tags
Does NOT use the invalid `{# … #}` Liquid comment syntax anywhere in code samples
States that the code belongs in `_layouts/post.html` (or a clearly-scoped include used by post.html)
Either uses tag-overlap manually OR mentions Jekyll's built-in `site.related_posts` with its `--lsi`/classifier-reborn cost
